title: Onboard a threat intelligence feed
question: How do you onboard a new threat intelligence feed into ServiceNow?
summary: Step-by-step feed intake and indicator governance flow.
scenario: Security leadership wants a new vendor feed imported and usable by analysts without creating noisy duplicates.
exactAnswer: Define the feed source, normalize the indicator fields, deduplicate incoming observables, apply confidence or severity metadata, and validate that the indicators enrich downstream investigations cleanly.
steps: Review the feed schema and indicator types.
Map external fields to the target indicator model.
Test a sample load and analyst review experience.
Confirm downstream enrichment works as expected.
commonMistakes: Loading every raw indicator without normalization or retention rules.

title: Convert a high-confidence alert into a security incident
question: How do you implement the handoff from a high-confidence security alert into a security incident workflow?
summary: Operational design for controlled alert-to-incident routing.
scenario: The SOC wants only high-confidence alerts to create formal security incidents with enrichment attached.
exactAnswer: Filter alert inputs, enrich them with context, create the incident only when the confidence threshold is met, assign ownership, and map the created record to the incident response lifecycle.
steps: Define the high-confidence threshold and source criteria.
Create the security incident only when the threshold is met.
Assign the record to the correct analyst queue.
Validate state transitions and analyst workflow.
validationChecklist: The created incident includes context and owner assignment.
commonMistakes: Creating incidents from every alert without triage logic.

title: Use vulnerability groups for remediation ownership
question: How do you implement vulnerability grouping so remediation teams receive actionable work?
summary: Grouping design for vulnerability ownership and remediation tracking.
scenario: The vulnerability team receives too many individual findings and wants grouped remediation work by application or owner.
exactAnswer: Define grouping criteria such as assignment group, CI owner, or application, then create remediation work from the group level instead of from every vulnerable item individually.
steps: Define grouping logic that matches remediation ownership.
Validate the groups produced from sample findings.
Route the group to the remediation owner.
Track closure at the group and item levels.
validationChecklist: Groups align to real ownership boundaries.
Remediation teams receive fewer, more actionable work items.
commonMistakes: Grouping findings in a way that crosses too many owners.
